description.p: Missingno is a Game Boy emulator with an integrated debugger. Manage your game library with automatic cover art, play history tracking, and save management. Play with keyboard or controller support, fullscreen mode, and multiple display palettes.
The built-in debugger provides code disassembly with breakpoints, CPU state inspection, VRAM visualization, audio channel inspection, and an interrupt viewer.

screenshot.caption: Game library with cover art and play history
Game details with activity log and save management
Playing a Game Boy game with Super Game Boy colours
The integrated debugger showing CPU state, disassembly, and VRAM

ul.li: New game library with cover art from Hasheous, play history, and save management
Per-game activity log with session tracking and zstd-compressed save snapshots
Settings screen with display options, rebindable keyboard and controller controls
Super Game Boy colour toggle with authentic SNES CRT gamma correction
Sharp bilinear screen scaling for crisp pixels at any resolution
One-click gbtrace frame capture from the debugger
Continued PPU accuracy improvements: pixel pipeline, DFF timing, HALT wakeup
Major accuracy improvements to the PPU pixel pipeline, APU, and memory bank controllers
New headless debugger mode with an HTTP API (--headless flag)

description.p: Initial release with core emulation, debugger, Super Game Boy support, controller support, save RAM, and fullscreen mode.
